knowledge_objects.name: Random Variable
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A function that assigns a numerical value to each outcome of a random experiment.
knowledge_objects.source_span: A random variable is a function that assigns a numerical value to each outcome of a random experiment.

knowledge_objects.name: Probability Distribution
knowledge_objects.short_definition: Describes how probability is assigned to the possible values of a random variable.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The probability distribution of a random variable describes how probability is assigned to its possible values.

knowledge_objects.name: Expected Value
knowledge_objects.short_definition: A weighted average of the possible values of a random variable.
knowledge_objects.source_span: The expected value of a random variable is a weighted average of its possible values.

knowledge_objects.name: Expected Value Formula (Discrete)
knowledge_objects.short_definition: For a discrete random variable X, E[X] = sum_x x P(X=x).
knowledge_objects.source_span: For a discrete random variable X, the expected value is E[X] = sum_x x P(X=x).

knowledge_objects.name: Variance
knowledge_objects.short_definition: Measures how far a random variable typically is from its expected value.
knowledge_objects.source_span: Variance measures how far a random variable typically is from its expected value.

knowledge_objects.name: Conditional Probability
knowledge_objects.short_definition: Measures the probability of an event given that another event has occurred.
knowledge_objects.source_span: Conditional probability measures the probability of an event given that another event has occurred.

knowledge_objects.name: Bayes' Rule
knowledge_objects.short_definition: Rewrites conditional probability as P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B).
knowledge_objects.source_span: Bayes' rule rewrites conditional probability in a form that is useful for updating beliefs: P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B).
